_instructions: This is the schema Claude Code follows when writing a paper profile. Fill every field. Use null for genuinely absent fields, never omit them. String values should be concise (1-2 sentences max) unless noted otherwise.
title: Full paper title
author: Last name(s) only

opening_move.type: One of: normative_puzzle | empirical_anomaly | conceptual_gap | polemical_intervention | interpretive_proposition | reconstructive_diagnosis
opening_move.description: 1-2 sentences: what problem or question opens the paper and how it is framed
anchored_in: List of thinkers/texts invoked to establish the puzzle
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Why does this paper say resolving this matters

rivals_engaged: List of named positions or authors the paper explicitly argues against or with
theoretical_positioning.engagement_structure: One of: dialectical_contrast | sustained_critique | friendly_amendment | absence_critique | no_rivals_engaged
theoretical_positioning.own_position_type: One of: middle_way | internal_reconstruction | external_alternative | synthesis | none
theoretical_positioning.description: 1 sentence: how the paper positions itself relative to existing literature

epistemic_mode.primary: One of: fully_normative | normative_with_interpretive_case | normative_with_qualitative_empirical | normative_with_quantitative_empirical | mixed_empirical_normative | primarily_empirical_qualitative | primarily_empirical_quantitative | purely_interpretive_hermeneutic
epistemic_mode.secondary: A second mode if clearly present, else null
epistemic_mode.empirical_evidence_role: One of: constitutive | illustrative | supportive | absent — what function does empirical material serve in the argument?
epistemic_mode.quantitative_methods: Describe if present (regression, survey, content analysis, etc.) else null
epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: Describe if present (interviews, ethnography, discourse analysis, etc.) else null
data_sources: List any named datasets, archives, or empirical sources used, else empty list
epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Describe if present (hermeneutic, deconstruction, genealogy, etc.) else null
epistemic_mode.notes: Any important nuance about how evidence and argument relate in this paper

case_type.case_medium: One of: film | literary_text | historical_event | legal_case | political_event | ethnographic_field | statistical_dataset | philosophical_text | none
case_type.case_name: Name of the specific case used, or null
case_type.case_function: One of: parable_for_theory | test_case_for_framework | counter_example | generative_of_theory | illustration | primary_object_of_analysis | none
case_type.case_relation_to_argument: One of: illustrative_and_generative | illustrative_only | evidence_for_claim | object_of_interpretation | none

normative_stake.level: One of: high | medium | low | absent — how explicitly normative is the paper's conclusion?
normative_stake.domain: e.g. democratic theory | justice | recognition | sovereignty | methodology
normative_stake.claim_type: One of: prescriptive | critical_diagnostic | reconstructive | interpretive | descriptive
normative_stake.closure_type: One of: strong_normative_conclusion | open_ended_with_burden_on_reader | aporetic_suspension | research_agenda | policy_recommendation | none

move_sequence: Ordered list of rhetorical/argumentative moves. Use functional labels from this vocabulary:
IMPORTANT: this must be a plain list of label strings, not descriptions. E.g.:
_reflexive_note: true if the paper explicitly qualifies, critiques, or acknowledges limits of its own argument
key_concepts: Up to 6 concepts that are central to THIS paper's argument (not the field generally)

central: Thinkers whose frameworks are actively used to build the argument

confidence_notes: Any uncertainty about the classification — e.g. the paper's epistemic mode is ambiguous because X, or the move sequence is hard to determine because Y
